name: science-research
description: Models ONE Science Agent research iteration (GENERATE->TEST->REVIEW->INTEGRATE->REPORT) from ds-design.md. A planner (thread) proposes 3 discriminating angles; isolated branch workers explore them in parallel, appending structured BranchResults; an evidence critic reviews quality/conflicts at a barrier; an integrator maps evidence to hypotheses and confidence bands; a reporter synthesizes the final report. The design multi-iteration convergence loop lives in the outer Harness (which re-invokes this graph per iteration with a fresh frontier), not in accumulating graph state.

planner.description: GENERATE: propose 3 distinct, discriminating investigation angles from the research question and prior integration (if any).
planner.prompt:
You are the research Planner. Given the research question and the prior integration summary (if any from a previous iteration), propose exactly 3 DISTINCT investigation angles labeled A, B, C.

Each angle must either target one specific hypothesis or discriminate between competing hypotheses. Prefer angles whose results would change the conclusion.

Research question:
{{input.task}}

Prior integration (if any):
{{integration}}

Return a concise plan: for each of A/B/C, one sentence on what to investigate and which hypothesis it bears on.

branch_a.description: TEST angle A in an isolated session.

branch_b.description: TEST angle B in an isolated session.

branch_c.description: TEST angle C in an isolated session.

evidence_critic.description: REVIEW: at the barrier, assess evidence quality, conflicts, and limitations across all branch results.
evidence_critic.prompt:
You are the Evidence Critic. Three branch workers returned results:

{{branch_results}}

Review for: (1) evidence quality and directness, (2) conflicts between branches, (3) key limitations. Do NOT invent new evidence; only assess what's present.

Return JSON with EXACTLY: {"strongest_evidence": ["..."], "conflicts": ["..."], "key_limitations": ["..."], "quality_assessment": "<one paragraph>"}.

integrator.description: INTEGRATE: map reviewed evidence to hypotheses, assign confidence bands, decide whether evidence is sufficient to report.
integrator.prompt:
You are the Evidence Integrator. Map the reviewed evidence onto the competing hypotheses, assign each hypothesis a confidence band, and decide whether the evidence is sufficient to produce a final report.

Reviewed evidence:
{{reviewed_evidence}}

Confidence threshold for reporting: {{research.confidence_threshold}}.

Return JSON with EXACTLY: {"hypotheses": [{"claim": "...", "confidence": "unsupported|weak|plausible|moderate|strong", "net_support": "supports|contradicts|inconclusive"}], "leading": "<the currently best-supported claim>", "sufficient": <boolean: true if the leading hypothesis is 'moderate' or 'strong' AND no unresolved fatal conflict remains; otherwise false>, "open_questions": ["..."]}. Be conservative: weak or conflicting evidence means sufficient=false so the loop can refine.

reporter.description: REPORT: synthesize the final research report from the integrated hypotheses and reviewed evidence.
